entry.title: New System Based Certifications for SAP S/4HANA Sales – Now Available!
entry.content:
These updated exams consist of real, hands‑on tasks executed directly in an SAP system. The result: a much stronger focus on applied skills and real‑world capability. In the area of Sales, we offer certifications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition – Sales and for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition – Sales.

How to Prepare Successfully

1. Explore the official Learning Journeys (free on learning.sap.com)

Public Edition – Sales
👉 https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition-sales
Private Edition – Sales
👉 https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sales-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition

These structured learning paths guide you through all essential topics included in the certification.

2. Get hands‑on practice

Use the available hands‑on training systems to run through exercises, demos, and end‑to‑end scenarios.
This practical experience is key for the new system‑based exam format. Hands-on systems can be found in the Learning Journeys.

3. Join the “Get Certified!” Live Sessions

SAP experts provide guidance, highlight important focus areas, and answer your questions — perfect for a confident exam day. Join the sessions or watch the recordings:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-implementation-consultant-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sales?searchId=63470ea3-be21-4301-97dc-1f476feecf10&listPosition=3

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-associate-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-sales

Why a system‑based certification?

Because real skills matter.
This new exam format demonstrates not only what you know, but what you can do inside SAP S/4HANA Sales.
A strong signal for customers, partners, and your own professional development.

Ready to boost your skills?

With Learning Journeys, hands‑on systems, and expert Live Sessions, you have everything you need to succeed in the new SAP S/4HANA Sales certifications in 2026.

entry.title: SAP Core Logistics for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, 2025 FPS00 & FPS01 – What’s New
entry.content:
We are proud to introduce the first-ever “What’s New” release blog for Core Logistics in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, highlighting the 2025 FPS00 and FPS01 releases. This update has been a long time coming, and it reflects sustained commitment across our teams to bring durable, enterprise-grade capabilities to our customers. Despite significant challenges over the past years, our dedicated engineering teams have persisted in delivering a set of high-quality features and enhancements.

Core Logistics provides the foundational execution and planning capabilities that underpin end-to-end logistics processes in SAP S/4HANA. Within the broader SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain portfolio, Core Logistics sits at the center — providing the essential, reliable core that enables advanced planning, warehouse management, transportation management and industry-specific extensions to work together seamlessly. The 2025 release enhances that foundation with targeted improvements designed to increase operational resilience, simplify integration, and improve day-to-day usability for logistics professionals.

In the following sections, we’ll highlight the key innovations in this release, show how they deliver real-world value to customers, and provide resources to support implementation and adoption. We’re excited to share this milestone and grateful to the teams and customers who helped make it possible.

As I missed publishing this piece alongside the initial SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 release, and with the recent release of FPS01, I shall combine both releases into one single blog. And we shall begin this overview with the highlights delivered in FPS00.

One more important thing to note is that I shall group the delivered innovations according to their corresponding solution capability. For those new to Logistics Execution – or as a quick refresher - here are the key capability categories we’ll be exploring:

  • Delivery Management – a component of SAP Logistics Execution, Delivery Management is an important part of the logistics chain in which guaranteed customer service and distribution planning support play major roles. In Delivery Management, all delivery procedure decisions can be made at the start of the process by: (1) Considering general business agreements with your customer; (2) Recording special material requests; (3) Defining shipping conditions in the sales order. The result is an efficient and largely automatic shipping process in which manual changes are only necessary under certain circumstances.
  • Handling Unit Management – used to map packing-controlled logistics in the SAP system, HU Management does not consider the movements of the individual materials, instead it maps the movements of the handling units that contain these materials. Available in all core logistic areas where logistic material bookings can take place, this feature uses handling units to map logistical processes to simplify the processing of goods movements thus optimizing logistical processes. This simplified process includes the existing packing function across core logistics and the storage unit processing in the Warehouse Management system.
  • Inventory Management - Inventory Management is part of the Materials Management module and is fully integrated into the entire logistics system. Material is procured from external or internal sources based on the requirements determined by Material Requirements Planning. The delivery is entered in Inventory Management as a goods receipt. The material is stored (and managed under Inventory Management) until it is delivered to customers (Sales & Distribution), or is used for internal purposes (for example, for production).

CORE LOGISTICS IN SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00

Delivery Management

Supply to Customer (S2C) Integration

The first highlight I want to mention is one tightly linked to EWM. This is a foundational update which will allow users to send deliveries that are relevant for the Supply to Customer (S2C) scenario to EWM. Just-in-Time (JIT) processing is a common practice in manufacturing industries for efficient demand-driven production and logistics across supply chains. Just-In-Time Supply to Customer covers JIT processes from the perspective of a supplier. This update for Delivery Management enables production warehouse operators to work with the supply-to-customer (S2C) process for Just-In-Sequence (JIS) calls when using Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA in their storage locations.

After the production and packing of the sequenced component groups, the system creates outbound deliveries using the Managed Customer JIT Calls by Component Groups app, including handling unit (HU) numbers from (multi-level) packing. These deliveries are then transferred to EWM. PLEASE NOTE that on the EWM side this feature will be enabled as of the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release from February 2026.

Customizing updates in Delivery Management

In Customizing activity Activate Decentralized Warehouse System, users can choose how SAP S/4HANA communicates with the external Warehouse Management system. In addition, customizing activity of Activate Service-Based Communication has been removed.

Furthermore, Activate Decentralized Warehouse System has been extended with the table column Comm. External WM. In this new column, users can select SOAP Web Services to enable the external warehouse management services. This configuration was previously made in Customizing activity Activate Service-Based Communication, which has been removed to avoid redundancy.

Inventory Management

OData API: Material Document - Read, Create

The OData API Material Document - Read, Create (API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT) has been enhanced with the following new fields:

  • ReservationItemRecordType - In the case of a discontinued material in a reservation from a production order, the value provided here indicates whether the discontinued material or the follow-up material should be consumed (“[   ]” = The discontinued material should be consumed; “1” = The follow-up material should be consumed)
  • BatchbySupplier - The Supplier Batch field has been added. This is the number used to manage the batch on the supplier’s side.

SOAP API: Material Document – Create

The SOAP API Material Document - Create (MaterialDocumentCreateRequest_Async_In) has been enhanced with a new field ReservationItemRecordType. For reservations coming from production orders, it is now possible to indicate whether to use a discontinued material or its follow-up material when the goods movement are created (“[   ]” = The discontinued material should be consumed; “1” = The follow-up material should be consumed).

Handling Unit Management

Enabling Auxiliary Packaging Material in 3PL SOAP APIs

Third-party warehouse management APIs enable data exchange with warehouse management systems in external systems. The main use of these APIs is to communicate with third-party warehouse systems and logistics providers. These services can be used to send and receive goods from the external warehouse. In addition, it's possible to update and split outgoing deliveries.

With the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 release, the 3rd Party Logistics SOAP APIs has been enhanced to capture secondary packaging information in the field AdditionalPackaging within the Handling unit node. The following SOAP APIs have been updated to support this enhancement:

  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_SHIP_ORDER
  • WAREHOUSEINTERMEDIATEUPDATE_IN
  • WAREHOUSESTOCKTRANSFERRECEIPTA
  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_STOCK_TRANS
  • WAREHOUSESHIPPINGADVICE_IN

Both Customizing activities can be accessed in transaction SPRO under Logistics Execution à Integration of External Warehouse Systems.

Enabling Key User Extensibility at the Handling Unit Header in 3PL SOAP APIs

Another enhancement for 3rd Party Logistics APIs is that key user extensibility was enabled at the Handling Unit header in the 3PL SOAP APIs used for external warehouse management. The following SOAP APIs were enabled to support custom fields creation at Handling Unit header via the Key User Extensibility app:

  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_SHIP_ORDER
  • WAREHOUSEINTERMEDIATEUPDATE_IN
  • WAREHOUSESTOCKTRANSFERRECEIPTA
  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_STOCK_TRANS
  • WAREHOUSESHIPPINGADVICE_IN

To use these services, they must first be configured in the S/4HANA system. For this, SAP provides a services group and AIF scenario. For more information about configuring the services in the SOA system, see the related sources for External Warehouse Management Services and for Central Configuration.

Enhancement of Handling Unit API with ISO Codes for Units of Measurement

 A final highlight worth mentioning is the enhancement of the Handling Unit (HU) API, which now includes new fields to support ISO-compliant units of measurement.

 At the Handling Unit header level, the following fields have been enabled to support ISO codes for units of measurement.

:

Field name 

Description 

HndlgUnitTareVolumeUnitISOCode

Tare volume unit of measure of a handling unit in ISO code.

HndlgUnitTareVolumeUnitSAPCode

Tare volume unit of measure of a handling unit in SAP code.

HndlgUnitTareWeightUnitISOCode

Tare weight unit of measure of a handling unit in ISO code.

HndlgUnitTareWeightUnitSAPCode

Tare weight unit of measure of a handling unit in SAP code.

UnitOfMeasureDimensionISOCode

Dimension unit of measure in ISO code.

UnitOfMeasureDimensionSAPCode

Dimension unit of measure in SAP code.

VolumeUnitISOCode

Volume unit of measure ISO code.

VolumeUnitSAPCode

Volume unit of measure in SAP code.

WeightUnitISOCode

Weight unit of measure ISO code.

WeightUnitSAPCode

Weight unit of measure of in SAP code.

 

 At the Handling Unit item level, the following fields have been enabled to support ISO codes for units of measurement

:

Field name 

Description 

HndlgUnitQuantityUnitISOCode

Quantity unit of a handling unit in ISO Code.

HndlgUnitQuantityUnitSAPCode

Quantity unit of a handling unit in SAP Code.

 

CORE LOGISTICS IN SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

Delivery Management

Persistence of Asset Postings in Delivery Structure

With this feature, we have smoothed the process of managing assets across multiple decentralized warehouse locations. Previously, when assets were moved between decentralized warehouses, the asset reference information would sometimes get lost in translation during the delivery process. Consequently, without clear asset identification, the system would throw posting errors, forcing users to manually trace which specific company asset was being moved.

With the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release, asset references can be embedded directly into delivery documents from the get-go. Now, when an asset moves through the warehouse network, its unique identifier travels with it every step of the way, from the initial goods receipt to the final goods issue posting. For logistics managers, this translates to fewer system interruptions, reduced manual intervention, and most importantly, reliable asset tracking across your entire warehouse ecosystem.

To enable this feature, the delivery communication structure KOMDLGN is extended with the asset fields ANLN1 (main asset number) and ANLN2 (asset subnumber). These fields are populated from the goods movement data (MSEG) during delivery generation and persist in the delivery item data (LIPS). During post goods issue, the same fields are mapped back from the delivery to the goods movement interface to ensure that the asset information is available for final posting.

Enhanced Transfer of Control Dates to support Multistage Intercompany Sales and Stock Transfer Processes

The Transfer of Control (ToC) refers to the specific point in time when ownership or responsibility for goods officially shifts from one party to another. This is critical for revenue recognition and accounting purposes, as it determines when a sale can be recorded and when inventory responsibility changes hands.

When dealing with multistage intercompany sales or stock transfers, such as when goods pass through multiple legal entities before reaching their destination, tracking exactly when control transfers at each handoff point becomes exponentially more complicated.

To support multistage intercompany sales and stock transfer process, the Transfer of Control (ToC) Dates in the outbound delivery header has been enhanced by introducing four new internal ToC-Date groups directly in the header. These additional fields provide the granularity needed to precisely coordinate and document each transfer of control event as goods move between the various organizations involved in your multistage process. Whether you’re managing a three-way intercompany sale or routing stock through regional distribution entities, you now have the tools to accurately capture each ownership transition along the journey.

For more information on Multistage Intercompany Sales process and the Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer process, please read the following Help Portal documentation:

  • Multistage Intercompany Sales
  • Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer

Other Delivery Management feature enrichments

And lastly with regards to Delivery Management enhancements delivered with the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release, I would like to briefly mention the following extensibility updates:

  • RAP Object updates – two RAP Business Objects (BO) available in Delivery Management have been updated. For the RAP BO I_INBOUNDDELIVERYTP we enabled editing of the Putaway Quantity field, as well as enabled the use of the PostPartialGoodsReceipt action for inbound deliveries. For the I_OUTBOUNDDELIVERYTP RAP BO, the Picking Quantity field is now editable. Furthermore, RAP events are now called when a delivery is assigned to or removed from a shipment using transaction Create Shipment (VT01N) or Change Shipment (VT02N). This allows you to effectively use the BO with shipments.
  • Extensibility for Goods Movements – with this release we also allow users now to extend and change data in a delivery before goods movement is posted. For this, the Business Add-In Goods Movement Data Modification (LE_SHP_SPE_GM_CREATE) can be used. The BAdI can be found in the Customizing (SPRO) for Logistics Execution (LE) under Extended Warehouse Management Integration > Transfer and Inventory Management > Business Add-Ins (BAdIs).

For more information on SAP’s Clean Core strategy, and how our extensibility updates factor into it, please read the Clean Core extensibility white paper.

Inventory Management

Prevention of Goods Receipt Posting for Purchase Order Items with 'Delivery Completed' Indicator Set

This feature introduces a safeguard that prevents goods receipts from being posted against purchase order items already marked with the 'Delivery Completed' indicator (field ELIKZ). When an attempt is made to post a goods receipt for such items, the system displays a customizable message. If the message category is configured as 'Error Message', the system will halt the goods receipt posting entirely for any items where the 'Delivery Completed' indicator is set.

This enhancement is particularly valuable for organizations looking to maintain tighter control over their inventory records. It eliminates the risk of inadvertently posting additional receipts against purchase order lines that have already been closed out – a scenario that can lead to inventory discrepancies and reconciliation headaches down the line.

On the configuration side, administrators have full control over how the system responds to these posting attempts. A new message (message class: M7, message number: 506) has been introduced in customizing table T160M specifically for this purpose. The message category options include 'No Message', 'Information', 'Warning', and 'Error Message', with 'No Message' set as the default. For organisations wanting to enforce strict compliance and prevent any goods receipts against completed items, setting the message category to 'Error Message' will ensure the system blocks these postings outright.

Support for Multistage Intercompany Sales and Stock Transfer Processes in Inventory Management

Inventory Management (MM-IM) receives a significant capability boost with this release, now fully supporting goods issue and goods receipt processes within multistage intercompany sales and stock transfer scenarios.

The Customizing activity 'Assign Movement Types to Value Chain Process Steps' has been expanded to accommodate these multistage scenarios. Dedicated movement types are now available specifically for multistage intercompany sales and stock transfers, and administrators have the flexibility to customize these movement types to align with their organization’s specific operational requirements.

Furthermore, the system now handles timing mismatches in the transfer process. In real-world operations, goods don’t always move in a perfectly sequential manner – sometimes an inbound delivery gets confirmed at the receiving plant before all intermediate steps in the chain have been completed. Previously, this could cause system errors and processing bottlenecks. Now, the system allows temporary negative Valuated Stock in Transit at the receiving plant when goods transfer occurs ahead of goods receipt posting.

Warehouse Handling Unit field in Material Documents Overview

This release brings improved traceability for handling unit information directly into core inventory documentation. The Material Documents Overview app (App ID: F1077) now includes a dedicated Warehouse Handling Unit field (Technical name: EWM_HUIDENT, Type: /SCWM/DE_HUIDENT), giving users immediate visibility into handling unit details without needing to navigate between multiple screens or systems.

Behind the scenes, several objects have been enhanced to support the persistence of Warehouse Handling Unit Identifiers within Material Documents. This means that when handling unit information flows in from Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the system now retains that data directly in the material document record rather than losing it in translation between systems.

BAPI function modules that have been enhanced with warehouse handling unit information:

  • MAP2I_B2017_GM_ITEM_TO_IMSEG
  • MP2E_IMSEG_TO_2017_ITEM_CREATE

The IDoc Segment E1BP2017_GM_ITEM_CREATE1 has also been enhanced.

Furthermore, two Core Data Services (CDS) views in Inventory have been updated to include the Warehouse HU field, namely the Material Document Item view (I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2) and the Goods Movement view (I_GOODSMOVEMENTDOCUMENT).

The Warehouse Handling Unit field is populated in the material document only when it is explicitly passed through the BAPI, IDoc, or OData interfaces.

Other Core Logistics Updates

And since the Core Data Services view for Material Document Item has already been mentioned, I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2 has been enhanced with service document fields, namely:

  • ServiceDocument
  • ServiceDocumentItem
  • ServiceDocumentType

Lastly, I want to go over two Extensibility updates that were delivered with this release:

  • The OData API ‘Material Document - Read, Create’ (API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT) has been enhanced with Warehouse Handling Unit field. More specifically, the ‘Document Items’ entity contains a new property, ‘HandlingUnitExternalID’.
  • Key user extensibility has been enabled for the Packing Instruction Header through SAP GUI and Odata API. Users can create custom fields for packing instruction table PACKKP using business context PACKINGINSTRUCTION via Key user extensibility. The created fields can be managed via SAP GUI transactions POP1/POP2/POP3, as well as via the via the OData API API_PACKINGINSTRUCTION.

Artificial Intelligence: Using Joule in batch management

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 2025 FPS01 release, batch management gets a modern, conversational upgrade with this release through Joule integration. Users can now query batch master information using natural language, making navigation easier and more intuitive. Simply ask Joule to search the batch required for your need, using the batch master attributes, Joule fetches the batch master information based on the criteria provided in the prompt.

The natural language queries support several key fields, including batch number, material, plants, batch status, manufacturing date, shelf-life expiry date, and supplier. This makes it easier to pull up batch information whenever needed, such as to check expiry dates for quality purposes or verify supplier details for traceability requirements.

When deeper analysis or updates are required, Joule provides seamless navigation directly to the batch master object page within the Manage Batches app. From there, users can explore additional batch details, modify information as needed, or take further actions. This integration represents a step forward in making everyday inventory tasks more accessible and efficient through AI-assisted interaction.

FIND OUT MORE!

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information on Multistage Intercompany Sales process and the Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer process, please read the following Help Portal documentation:

  • Multistage Intercompany Sales
  • Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer

For more information on SAP’s Clean Core strategy please read the Clean Core extensibility white paper.

entry.title: Fixed asset migration for French company
entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to ask for support regarding fixed asset migration into SAP S4HANA cloud public edition via migration cockpit for a French company. 

We are migrating an asset which was acquired in February 2020, depreciation start date is 01/02/2020. 

Useful life 4 years:

 

Just to mention, we have 2 ledgers (0L as operational ledger and 2L as local ledger).

The master data was properly migrated. However we are having issues with the second part of migration - the fixed asset postings. In template for fixed asset-postings we have the posting key tab fulfilled as follows:

And the cumulative values tab:

With this file being simulated, we are receiving an error messages for 2L ledger (0L is successfully simulated):

-depreciation area 93 does not manage APC values

-acquisition value will become negative in depreciation area 93

I do not understand, how could the acquisition value become negative, if the acquisition cost is 848,00 and depreciation 847,99. There should be 1 cent left. 

This is the fixed asset in system:

Legacy data transfer settings:

And migration key date is 31.12.2025

The 92 and 93 depreciation areas are specific for companies operating in FR. Is there something wrong with the set-up? What should we change to be able to migrate the asset? 

Is there anyone who has experience with FR fixed asset migration?

Thanks in advance

Zuzana Gersiova

entry.title: Re: SAC :How to count a Dimension based on measure value ?
entry.content:
@egil63 

May be in 2 steps. First create a calculated measure using your measure condition like IF ( [revenue] > 10000,1,0). Next steps just aggregate this calculated measure counting the 1s.

 

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Question about best practices for clients in SAP docu...
entry.content:
Yes clients are meant for these kind of purposes. Clients provide a more lightweight way within our application to structure your data. Alternatively you can also use subaccounts to fully separate your data, so it depends on your preference. 

If you want to have different user groups for each of these projects, you can also define restriction policies based on the Client ID via IAS: https://help.sap.com/docs/document-ai/sap-document-ai/restriction-policies


Best regards,
Tobias

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to set a monthly frequency (1x) for a...
entry.content:
I've found the answer that could be implemented here so just wanted to update this here. 

In Signavio, the weekly pattern options can be misleading for monthly processes. The simulation engine actually relies on an annualized frequency, so the correct way to model a task that runs once per month is simply to set the start event’s frequency to 12 cases per year. Signavio documentation confirms that simulation requires specifying the frequency per year for start events.

If your version forces weekly input, you can approximate this by selecting “full week” and entering 0.23 cases per week (12 ÷ 52).

However, using the annual frequency is the intended and more accurate method.

Hope this helps 🙂

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter March 2026
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here a preview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Getting Started with the New SAP Concur Travel for business users

Discover how the new SAP Concur Travel makes planning and booking work trips easier while helping you stay aligned with your company’s travel program. In just a short read, see what’s changing, how it supports your travelers, and how to get enabled along the way.

Full blog

Ready to Upgrade SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition?

Don’t let myths hold you back! Many teams hesitate to upgrade SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition due to common misconceptions—like being "too many releases behind" or fearing major disruptions. But what if upgrades could be seamless, strategic, and even empower your business?

Learn more

Meet our experts from SAP Enterprise Support to get updates

Join our session on March 25 to meet our experts from SAP Enterprise Support Academy and SAP Enterprise Support Advisory program. Get latest insights about the move to learning.sap.com and learning highlights and hear which workstream the ESAC innovation program offers in Q1.

Stay tuned and join on You Tube

See how SAP is transforming Ariba into an AI‑native source‑to‑pay platform

SAP is reimagining Ariba as an AI‑native, modern source‑to‑pay platform designed to simplify daily work, improve decision-making, and streamline procurement-to-pay processes. Read the blog to see practical examples, product highlights, and what these changes mean for faster cycle times, stronger cost control, and lower risk across your organization.

Read the full blog

New SAP for Me Services & Support dashboard-now live

We’ve launched a redesigned, user‑centric Services & Support overview tab to make support faster and easier. Enjoy a prominent search, actionable cards for Manage Cases, Quick Access (CIC), service requests and recommendations, unified case management, and simplified navigation — all designed to speed resolutions, boost self‑service, and improve outcomes.

entry.title: Accelerate Your SAP Digital Manufacturing Implementation with the Customer Onboarding Journey
entry.content:
Starting your SAP Digital Manufacturing project is an exciting milestone—but it also comes with many moving parts. To support you from the very beginning and help you build a strong foundation for your implementation, you have a Customer Onboarding Journey created specifically for SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Your central starting point is the SAP Digital Manufacturing Onboarding Resource Center. This hub brings together everything you need to move confidently through the early stages of your project. Inside, you’ll find:

  • Practical guidance on navigating SAP for Me
  • Instructions on how to access your SAP Digital Manufacturing system
  • A clear view of what happens during each phase of your project
  • Recommendations to prepare your team for a successful implementation

We also invite you to join the Onboarding Webcasts—interactive, quarterly sessions where SAP experts walk you through the onboarding process, highlight key considerations, and answer your questions. Can’t attend live? Recordings are always available on demand.

Your 4‑Phase Onboarding Journey

Every resource within the SAP Digital Manufacturing Onboarding Resource Center is mapped to one of four phases. These phases guide you step‑by‑step from contract signature to Go‑Live, helping ensure clarity and structure at every point in your onboarding experience.

 

Phase 1. Get Started with SAP

This phase introduces the essentials you need to gain access to your systems and understand the initial communications from SAP.
You’ll become familiar with key SAP channels—most importantly SAP for Me—and learn where to go when you need support.

Main topics include:

  1. Overview of your onboarding journey
  2. SAP channels & S‑user setup
  3. System access
  4. SAP Support fundamentals

Phase 2. Plan Your Implementation

Here, you’ll learn how SAP methodologies and tools help you structure and manage your SAP Digital Manufacturing project. This phase gives you insight into how to organize your project team, make use of SAP best practices, and establish an effective implementation plan.

Main topics include:

  1. Using SAP Activate for project delivery
  2. Managing your implementation with SAP Cloud ALM
  3. Offerings and tools that support your project
  4. Roles, responsibilities & testing considerations

Phase 3. Get Enabled

This phase is all about building capability. You’ll discover a broad range of free learning offerings designed to help your team understand and work effectively with SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Main topics include:

  1. Key SAP Digital Manufacturing features
  2. Enablement channels and learning resources
  3. Opportunities to engage with SAP experts and the community

Phase 4. Prepare to Go Live

As your project moves closer to Go‑Live, this phase helps you prepare for the transition into productive use. It outlines the key activities, support channels, and innovation tools available to you as you get ready to operate SAP Digital Manufacturing in a live environment.

Main topics include:

  1. Final preparations before Go‑Live
  2. Post Go‑Live support options
  3. Strategies for continuous improvement

Key Takeaways

The SAP Digital Manufacturing Customer Onboarding Journey is purpose‑built to support you through each step of your implementation. It equips you with clear guidance, curated resources, and access to expert support—helping you move efficiently from contract signature to Go‑Live.

Explore the resources, interact with the content, and take advantage of the tools designed to accelerate your success.

Your journey to a successful SAP Digital Manufacturing Go‑Live begins here.

entry.title: WBS asset settlement - not matching amount for 2025, CJI3 is not cleared
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have WBS for capex project from 2025. The report CJI3 shows that balance is not cleared. The last settlement was in 10.2025 and the balance after that was 0.0. But for period 10 the settlement was to AuC with rule/parameter -> FXA category, settlement receiver is asset under construction, 100% and AUC.  But later the client did another settlement from AuC to fixed asset, but the settlement rule was not FULL with 100%, but FXA, full and they provided amount. The difference of value between auc and fixed asset is the same as remaining balance in cji3.  When I did the summary per G/L account I see that the account for settlement shows the same amount as AuC (95k eur), but the value of asset is (92) because in the second settlement from auc to asset acquisition the client gave amount not percentage.. As the fiscal year 2025 is closed, how can we fix it to settle completely this WBS? It's S4 HANA core 107. I know that in S4 Hana cross-year settlement for capex is not a proper way. I would appreciate your guidance, ideas how to fix that issue.

entry.title: UI5con Bengaluru 2026 - Community, Code, and the Future of SAP UI Development
entry.content:
UI5con Bengaluru 2026 brought together 400+ developers, architects, consultants, and students from across India for a full day of learning, hands-on exploration, and community connection. Held at the SAP Labs campus in Devanahalli as part of SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2026, the event marked a historic double first — the first time UI5con has taken place in India in seven years, and the very first SAP Inside Track and UI5con held jointly at this location. The event covered everything from the cutting-edge world of AI-assisted development to the fundamentals of accessibility and enterprise UX — all through the lens of SAPUI5 and SAP Fiori.

This was more than a conference; it was a homecoming. With UI5con returning to India after seven years and joining forces with SAP Inside Track for the very first time, the energy in the room was something special — a community reunited and ready to build.

"It's my first SAP Inside Track session as an ABAP Fiori developer. I had a great session attending UI5con. Learned some amazing things during the session."
Participant, Deloitte USI

Kickoff: Agentic Coding with UI5 — From Idea to Inclusive App

Stefan Beck, Peter Muessig, and Rajashree Raghavendra opened the day with a keynote that was equal parts history lesson and vision statement — reminding the audience that UI5con Bengaluru began in 2018, grew to 400+ attendees, 28 sessions, and 41 speakers in 2019, and is now back after a COVID-forced pause of seven years. They traced SAPUI5’s journey from its 2012 enterprise foundations to its current form: TypeScript, Web Components, CI/CD, and a 90% accessibility score in the Web Almanac 2025. Two strategic bets anchored the roadmap: UI5 Web Components (framework-free, standards-based) and Legacy-Free OpenUI5/SAPUI5 (deprecated code removed, async by default).

The AI portion of the keynote charted the evolution from basic code completion in 2020 to the agentic coding era of today and introduced the UI5 MCP Server and Fiori MCP Server as the practical bridges between AI tools and SAP development best practices. A live demo closed the session — an inclusive app built from scratch using Fiori elements Building Blocks and Freestyle code, guided by agentic AI in real time. The vision set the tone for everything that followed: "UI5 in 2026 is where AI acceleration, inclusive design, and enterprise trust meet."

 

AI Takes Centre Stage

If there was one theme that ran through every session at UI5con 2026, it was AI — and not just as a buzzword. The hands-on session on the MCP Server for Fiori, led by Vinay Hospete, Ashish Anand, and Maheeth Reddy, introduced attendees to the Model Context Protocol — the emerging standard that connects AI coding agents to domain-specific tools and best practices. The demo pod on Vibe Coding with SAP UI5 brought this to life, showing how natural language prompts combined with Cline and SAP AI Core enable governed, auditable UI5 app generation — where AI accelerates the work, but the developer stays in control.

"Vibe coding: Using the MCP server, the code can be generated quickly and can be adapted for the demo & quick mockup."
Participant, Nokia

 

Deep Dives: Going Beyond the Basics

The deep dive track gave attendees space to go further. Merlin Beutlberger's UI5 in the World of AI showcased the UI5 MCP Server's 10 tools — from app creation and API reference lookups to Linter runs and TypeScript migration guides — with a live demo of an AI agent building a full UI5 app from a natural language prompt. Jitendra Kansal's MDK session addressed how to bring Fiori to mobile without a rewrite, with the MDK MCP Server intelligently transforming UI for smaller screens. Mohammed Sijas introduced the DataRepository library, a lightweight solution that brings OData-like CRUD convenience to plain REST APIs.

Sandeep R S and Vinay Hospete reinforced that ~90% of SAP's own SAPUI5 apps are built on Fiori elements, and that MCP-powered agentic AI is the natural next step. Syed's session on Fiori Applications Demystified gave backend developers and architects a grounding in MVC, bindings, and when to choose Freestyle over Fiori Elements.

"Good place to know what's new and what is the future scope. Hands-on sessions give good insight on the topics."
Participant, Cargill

 

Accessibility, Web Components, and More

Gergana Georgieva's session on UI5's Ever-Evolving Accessibility was a highlight for many attendees. She walked through SAP's commitment to WCAG 2.2 and ARIA 1.2 standards, covering everything from keyboard navigation and screen reader support to the new Target Size requirements. The key message: accessibility requires both framework support and developer discipline — it must become a habit, not an afterthought.

"I really liked the accessibility session."
Participant, Wipro

The demo pod on UI5 Web Components — presented by Rounak, Vignesh, and Dharshini — showcased the growing maturity of the framework-agnostic component library built on web standards. Web Components 2.0 was announced, with new components including the intriguing <ui5-ai-button>. For developers working with React, Angular, or Vue, this opens new doors.

"It was very interactive. Now they are releasing web components 2.0 which looks promising."
Participant, Adidas

The Suite Controls session from Ashwin Kumar Bolar, Vinay K N, and Santhosh covered enterprise-grade components for complex business applications: Network Graph, UploadSetWithTable, and the Gantt Chart — each purpose-built for the kind of rich operational dashboards that SAP customers depend on. Kotresh T M rounded out the day with a deep dive into cross-app navigation for Fiori microfrontends, making the case for intent-based navigation as the standard pattern for loosely coupled Fiori apps.

 And for those excited about the future of AI in SAP applications, Arun Bharathi K's demo on Joule Frontend Actions showed how UI5 apps can expose capabilities directly to SAP's digital AI assistant — making Joule a conversational interface for everyday Fiori interactions.

"Got to know about the capability of Joule with the frontend actions. Would like to have a blog on this."
Participant, AMGBT

 

Hands-On Sessions: Learning by Doing

A major draw of UI5con has always been its hands-on sessions — and 2026 was no exception. Attendees rolled up their sleeves and built real UI5 apps, working through exercises on SAP Fiori elements V4, the Flexible Programming Model, and the UI5 Linter, with GitHub repositories shared so participants could take the work home. Volunteers circulated the room throughout, ensuring no one got stuck, and the atmosphere — equal parts focused and buzzing — was one of the highlights of the day.

“Attended 2 hands-on sessions of UI5con. Volunteers are always ready to help, amazing knowledgeable speakers. Loved the UI5con event.”
Participant, Deloitte

 

Ask the Expert: The Closing Highlight

The day closed with one of its most anticipated moments: the Ask the Expert panel. Peter Muessig, Vinod S Nair, Stefan Beck and Sandeep R S — some of the most prominent names behind UI5 and SAP Fiori — took to the stage together for an open, free-flowing conversation with the audience. The session was part Q&A, part fireside chat, and completely candid. Attendees asked about the roadmap, the future of legacy migrations, how best to adopt agentic AI in their own teams, and much more. It was exactly the kind of direct access to the people building the platform that makes UI5con special — and a fitting send-off for the day.

 

The Community Makes the Event

Across the feedback forms, one theme stood out as clearly as the technical content: people valued the community. Volunteers were praised for their patience, speakers for making complex topics accessible, and the hands-on format — with GitHub links, live demos, and side-by-side coding — gave participants something concrete to take home. Attendees also shared candid suggestions: better mic coverage in demo pods, clearer guidance during hands-on sessions, and more depth on MCP server configuration. All noted, and all on the list for next time.

 

Looking Ahead

UI5con Bengaluru 2026 made one thing clear: seven years was too long to wait. The SAP UI5 ecosystem is moving faster than ever, the community is hungry for it, and Devanahalli proved it can host a world-class developer event. Thank you to every speaker, volunteer, and attendee who made this happen. See you at the next one.

"Looking forward for the next iteration of UI5con."
Participant, Volvo

entry.title: Re: How to automate or simplify the mass creation of loca...
entry.content:
Hello @bryan_mendoza71 

Thank you for your question.

The Public Cloud does not offer standard mass function to automatically create and assign Local Dependencies for KMAT BOM items the way you described. The classic BOM and dependency editor flow is still largely manual per item.

See the following reference for more details. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/82cc8757c483ad6be10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

That said you can streamline the work significantly by changing how you model and assign dependencies. This will ensure you avoid creating 169 local dependencies.

To reduce the effort on your side, I suggest you:

  • Use Global Dependencies and reuse them instead of creating many local ones.
  • Centrally create and Maintain dependencies VC modelling Environment, then assign them where you need.

See the following reference for more details. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/ebe7d3f000ee45e7b2d3ffb6d893b5ee.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/ff8cbc37e1a849cfbb9eee9cb6d2548d.html?locale=en-US

A Public Cloud best practice to help you streamline is:

  • Avoid local dependencies and reuse global dependencies. IF your only driver is “we want a dependency per item”, consider modelling the logic as global selection conditions and then assign them to the BOM items. This ensures you create fewer dependencies, massively reduce manual steps and you can create versions and reuse logic cleanly.
  • A second option is to use BOM mass maintenance to reduce clicks. However, it is primarily for BOM item data, not a full replacement for dependency creation and assignment. Use it where it applies (for example item category, validity, quantity, etc.) and keep dependencies minimal and reusable.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/09ef824d11d846ccb9e46c5eb2150a06.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/702c12d24ac34a37b7a6bacfd6bd6176.html?locale=en-US

To summarise:

  • In standard Public Cloud, you cannot create mass local dependencies for BOM items in the flow you showed.
  • Suggested best optimization is for you to model dependencies centrally in VC Modelling Environment and reuse global dependencies.
  • Use BOM mass maintenance for repeated item fields changes but do not expect it to replace dependency creation.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP PS non valuated project Stock.
entry.content:
1. Account Assignment Q vs. P
Non-valuated project procurement can be done using either Q (Material) or P (Project) account assignment, but the system behavior differs.
• With Q account assignment, the material is received into a project-specific stock (special stock). The stock remains valuated and is available until it is issued.
• With P account assignment, no stock is created at all. At Goods Receipt, the value is posted directly to the project expense account. In other words, the material is treated as immediately consumed and charged to the project without creating inventory.


2. Documents Created at Goods Receipt (GR)
For a PO with P account assignment, the following documents are generated at GR:
• Material document (via MIGO)
• Accounting document posting directly to the project expense account (generally 6xxx range) along with GR/IR
• Project cost line items, which can be viewed in CJR2 or CJPJ
Since no stock is maintained, there is no inventory update.


3. MBBS Report Visibility
Non-valuated project procurement (P account assignment) will not appear in the MBBS report after GR because MBBS only shows valuated inventory balances.
However, the posted costs can still be reviewed in:
• CJR2 – Project Actual Costs
• CJPJ – Project Line Items
• MKPF/MSEG – Material document details

 

Regards,

Varun Maheshwari

entry.title: Re: Need Approval workflow for Depreciation Run
entry.content:
Hi @Devi_Narasimha1 ,
Yes you right, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud does not provide a standard approval workflow for the Depreciation Run, and there's no native workflow exists for this process in the Public Cloud edition

As you know the depreciation run is a periodic run and system-driven posting (scheduled via the Schedule Depreciation Run app), SAP does not allow workflow interception before posting. However, you can build an approval around the process using:

Business Process Configuration (SSCUI)
Flexible Workflow (for custom objects)
Custom Business Objects (CBO)
BTP Workflow / Build Process Automation
Two‑person rule or segregation of duties
Job scheduling controls

Let me know if this helps you.

entry.title: Re: Authtorization for Manage Bank Statement
entry.content:
Hi,
My friend the key point is that SAP Fiori App F1564 (Manage Bank Statements) does not use the classic FI authorization objects like F_BL_BANK to filter which bank statements a user can see. That’s why the object you tried has no effect.

This solution can work with you. Restrict access via Bank Account Master (recommended)
Assign authorization groups to House Bank Accounts and restrict users via below authorization objects mentioned below.

1. F_CLM_BAMR (Role-based access)
2. F_CLM_BAMH (House Bank)
3. F_CLM_BAMT (Account Type)

This will indirectly restrict which bank statements appear in F1564.

Please accept the solution is it work with you.

entry.title: SAP Customer Loyalty Management
entry.content:
SAP Customer Loyalty Management is our next generation solution to drive increased customer engagement and superior customer lifetime value. 

SAP Customer Loyalty Management focuses on 1st party data to enable pinpoint targeting of customers with offers and rewards to achieve loyalty goals.

It is one system which can operate across regions and brands to deliver true holistic loyalty across any size and shape enterprise.

It enables easy inclusion of partners outside of the organisation so loyalty programs can be even more valuable to consumers and customers increasing brand relevance.

It delivers native integration into S/4HANA for liability and accounting across all brands and regions enabling you to see the value of loyalty programs and the inherent business benefits they deliver. 

Great News! - A Test and Demo licence is now available on the Partner Pricing App.

This test and demo package contains all the functionality of the main solution, including  full UI and API access to build custom demonstrations.

Come and help deliver the next wave of CX growth in your organisation by becoming experts in SAP Customer Loyalty Management. 

Supplemental terms and conditions are available on the trust center. You can find this solution in the partner pricing app, SKU #8021412.

entry.title: Credit check result details not shown with error reaction at delivery (SAP Cloud ERP)
entry.content:
Hello Community, 

We are using credit check at the delivery step and our reaction type is D (Like B-with exceeded limit) at the configuration 101518-Define Automatic Credit Control. 

But while creating delivery at public cloud with this configuration do not give the details for error message. We need obstruct the saving of delivery.  

Already there is a note for S/4HANA  and solution is to Modifying the method if_ex_badi_sd_cm~fscm_display_credit_messages, but we need this modification for public cloud project. And looking from another screeen and calculating the limits is not a feasible solution. We need option 2. This is a important step for our go-live date.   

Here the note and community link for S/4HANA solution: 

SAP community answer: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/static-credit-check-during-delivery-not-showing-amount-exceeded-fscm/qaq-p/12670231 

Sap note: https://me.sap.com/notes/2871140 

--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  1. Create credit check relevant sales order/delivery document 
  1. Try to save the document 
  1. Receive VM010 error message: "Credit check SAP Credit Management failed Credit Segment xxxx of partner yyyyy" 

Thanks in advance for your support!

BR,
Evrim

fyi @hasanbayram  @yusufavci @dogaozdogan 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud UM Modeling Performance Improvement Tips and Tricks
entry.content:
SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud UM) empowers users to design content‑based applications that efficiently handle high‑volume data calculations and complex processing requirements. By leveraging its flexible and scalable modeling capabilities, SAP PaPM Cloud UM allows organizations to transform large datasets into actionable insights while maintaining performance and reliability.

When it comes to performance, it’s no longer just a nice-to-have but it has become an absolute necessity. In SAP PaPM Cloud UM, achieving faster calculation runs is a key factor in ensuring efficient memory usage, enhancing user experience, and allowing for better data handling as business data continues to grow over time.

So, with all that in mind, this blog post will walk you through some of the performance improvement tips and tricks for SAP PaPM Cloud UM based on existing documentation, smarter data model design, making the most out of parallelization.

To clearly outline the key areas of performance optimization, I’ve organized the discussion into sections. This approach also allows you to easily navigate to the topics most relevant to your specific use case.

  1. General Performance Recommendations
  2. Capacity Sizing
  3. Parallelization
  4. Modeling Optimization
  5. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Let us proceed.

I.        General Performance Recommendations

  • HANA SDA is a compelling synchronous remote access feature, but because of that nature, reading or writing fails immediately if the communication with the source system is disrupted for a microsecond, the remote system is down for maintenance, some authorization tokens get expired or access control changes. Therefore, it should be used only in exceptional cases.

    When reading from HANA SDA (Smart Data Access) sources, consider the following: Create statistics for an SDA Virtual table as described in Create Statistics on a Virtual Table or Linked Database | SAP Help Portal.

  • If the Manage Event Logs application has too many entries, you may consider performing event log archiving and deletion. For more information, see Manage Archives.

    You can also maximize archiving via schedule by following the steps described in SAP PaPM Cloud UM’s Event Log Archive and Deletion using SAP Job Scheduling Service.

  • Verify whether the HANA DB parameters have been set correctly as described in SAP Note 2186744. If you have set the STATEMENT_MEMORY_LIMIT parameter in the system, the recommended value is 30% of the global allocation limit (if you have 1 HANA node of 3TB, then it should be at 30% of 3TB, which is approximately 1TB).

  • Use the Manage Event Logs application to analyze the runtime performance KPIs for your process. In the detail screen of the Event Log, you can find information about the time spent on input reading, business processing, and writing to the database for each involved function. You can also find information on the number of processed entries and the allocated memory.

    For more information, see Manage Event Logs.

II.        Capacity Sizing

  • Sizing: Incorrect sizing could also cause memory issues. Perform sizing exercises at different points throughout the project (blueprint, realization, final preparation, go-live, change requests/functional changes post go-live, etc.) and not just once during initial phases. Incorrect sizing would also lead to out-of-memory/performance issues (despite performance tuning of the model) because the available hardware resources would not be enough to run the calculations with a high volume of data. You could consider engaging Expert Sizing Consulting.

  • Before going to production, perform a performance test such as a load/scalability/volume test on a preproduction system. You can use incremental volumes up to production-like volumes to see if the configured model scales linearly, and to get an idea of how much time/memory it will take. This is necessary to ensure that you have performed all necessary performance tuning in your configured calculation model and to confirm whether the sizing of the production system is correct. In such tests, you might realize that the initial/theoretical sizing numbers are not applicable anymore, for example, in case the scenarios/data models/assumptions have changed. As a result, you may need a larger or smaller-sized production system based on the current realizations.

III.        Parallelization

  • The Manage Partitions application allows you to manage large datasets efficiently, optimize performance, and maintain data integrity to maximize your data management capabilities. In the Universal Model application, partitioning is the process of dividing a large dataset into smaller and more manageable pieces of information. This way, the application not only enhances the speed of data processing but also allows for better utilization of system resources.

    For more information, see Manage Partitions and SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model: What’s New as of 2024-10-07?

  • If your calculation model/environment involves writing high-volume data to many model tables and you have implemented packaging using the environment partitioning setting as mentioned in the previous step, consider partitioning these model tables. For SAP PaPM-generated tables (of Model Entity function or any other function's buffer result table), this can be done by maintaining the partitioning ID in the Writer, Partition settings and reactivating the function.

    This will improve the write/insert times considerably and thereby enhance the overall processing time.

  • Apply hash partitioning or, if required, a combined hash-range partitioning on all Writer functions. A suitable number of partitions is 100. In case the Writer writes into a Model Entity, apply the partitioning on the Model Entity. This will reduce the time to write data to database roughly by a factor of 2.

  • When using a Process, always assign a Writer as the "To Activity". With this setup, you can make use of applied hash partitioning to have a significant improvement in terms of performance since you cannot apply Hash Partitioning to other functions, for example Join function.

IV.        Modeling Optimization

  • Avoid unnecessary functions or reduce staging functions in between to reduce materialization of data, which will consume significant memory and lead to longer runtime. In addition, you can combine functions to achieve calculations on the already/same materialized data. Effectively try to reduce the number of functions in the overall environment/calculation model.

  • Maintain filters/selections as early as possible, and apply selections already in the Input tab, if possible, rather than in rules.

  • Reduce the number of columns/fields if they are not really needed in the data you process as early as possible, for example, in cases where most columns are not actually needed during the calculation but were just included for reporting purposes.

  • When modeling a calculation model in SAP PaPM Cloud UM based on previous/legacy solutions, avoid taking over the logic one-to-one. Instead, reduce the number of functions by using SAP PaPM Cloud UM standard functionalities.

  • Use iterative loops with caution and careful consideration (if there are no other SAP PaPM Cloud UM standard options available). Functions or chains of functions that are run within iterative loops should be limited as much as possible.

  • Avoid using nested iterative loops, if possible. Be aware that using nested iterative loops would cost significant time and memory, and the data would grow significantly over every iteration, sometimes exponentially if not carefully configured.

  • To run a function and its related input functions without saving results, set a condition that is never met. This approach saves writing time, especially with high-volume data. The system runs all functions in the input network, but the condition prevents function from selecting any entries, so no data is written to the database.

  • If possible, aggregate data, and reduce granularity as early as possible in the calculation model. This will reduce the number of records to be processed by the calculation model. Verify whether the business scenario/requirement really needs the detailed granularity level of data to be processed throughout the calculation model.

  • For allocation scenarios, check and compare the approach of an account-based allocation vs. a costing/value field-based allocation approach. In certain scenarios, a costing/value field-based allocation approach is more performant/feasible.

  • In an Allocation function, use allocation type “Allocation with detailed offset records” judiciously, and only if needed, as this type produces much more granular results than the other types, resulting in a greater number of records.

V.        Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

1.     Are partitioning settings implicitly applied to all upstream functions leading up to a Writer function that has partitioning configured?

Yes, Hash-Range Partitioning supports the run of the Writer function, including its upstream function chain in sequential levels of parallel range-packages, for example, to save HANA memory or to reduce/control the overall HANA workload, that is, partially sequential or leveled package parallelization.

2.     If partitioning is applied in the Writer function with an Allocation function as input, shall partitioning be applied to the Sender or Receiver input?

If you use Hash-Dynamic or Hash-Range partitioning on the underlying input which is your Model Entity, the reading speed can be improved. If this type of partitioning is applied to the Sender input, then only the Sender reading is improved. If the same partitioning is applied to the Receiver input, then Receiver reading is also improved.

3.    Will an Information function also apply “partitioning”, meaning the SQL statement will be run separately for each partition and then assembled in memory?

Yes, a separate SQL statement is processed per partition. These partitions process different records.

4.    Does partitioning require larger memory utilization for Information functions since it will have a separate SQL statement run for each partition?

Yes, running multiple partitions consumes larger memory utilization in exchange for faster processing time.

That’s all for now! Thank you for taking the time to read the Modeling Performance Improvement Tips and Tricks of SAP PaPM Cloud UM. I hope the insights shared help you enhance and optimize your SAP PaPM Cloud UM.

See you again in the next blog post!

entry.title: Real Estate/Contract & Lease Financial postings
entry.content:
We are implementing Contract & Lease in SAP Public Cloud for our Real Estate companies and are having difficulty understanding how the flow types should be set up to achieve deferred revenue and VAT postings without a fixed asset assignment. Does anyone know how the configuration should be set up for the scenario below?

The local GAAP requirement is as follows:

For property where you notify the rent with outgoing VAT (tenants who are liable for VAT), you must account for prepaid rents. The notices are issued in advance, and if they are also paid in advance, this affects the VAT return.

Example:

If a company has a property and is to notify for quarter 3, the notice is made in June with a due date of 30/6. Rents are normally to be paid in advance.

The rent is paid in advance.

If the tenant pays by the due date at the latest, the VAT must be reported in the VAT return for quarter 2, and the income must be booked as prepaid rents.

The rent is paid within the rental period.

If the tenant pays after the due date, but in the period to which the rent relates, the VAT must be reported in quarter 3. If the tenant in the above example pays the rent during the rental period, i.e. quarter 3, the VAT must be reported in the VAT return for quarter 3

The rent is paid after the rental period.

If the tenant pays after the rental period (quarter 3) in the example above, the VAT must still be reported in the VAT return for quarter 3. The same applies if the rent is not paid at all. The outgoing VAT is therefore always reported no later than the accounting period after the due date, since the rental period is then provided.

Regards

Annette

entry.title: Turning GRC into a business conversation: a new Learning Journey for Sales and Presales
entry.content:
The goal of Positioning the SAP Business Suite for Governance, Risk and Compliance is simple: help customer-facing teams explain GRC in business terms. Not as a compliance obligation, but as a way to protect revenue, reduce operational risk, and improve decision-making.

 Participants learn how embedded SAP GRC capabilities in SAP Business Suite enable continuous control monitoring, real-time risk visibility, and a shared risk view across finance, procurement, and IT. The focus is on what matters in customer conversations: earlier issue detection, less manual audit work, fewer errors, and a stronger operational backbone

The journey includes three courses that build the story step by step — from GRC fundamentals, to integration in the SAP landscape, to practical guidance on how to position the value in sales situations. A knowledge check at the end allows learners to earn a badge.

Why this matters for Learning and Enablement

If you work in learning, partner enablement, or capability development, this is where your role becomes critical.

Strong GRC positioning doesn’t scale on its own. It needs to be part of onboarding, role-based learning paths, and ongoing enablement. Many Sales and Presales colleagues are comfortable discussing core processes, but less confident when the conversation turns to risk, controls, or audit impact.

This Learning Journey helps close that gap.

Consider actively promoting it within your Sales and Presales communities, adding it to Business Suite learning paths, and sharing it with partners who work on S/4HANA Cloud and transformation opportunities. The earlier teams understand the business value of embedded GRC, the easier it becomes to bring the topic into customer discussions — not as a technical add-on, but as part of the core value story.

The Learning Journey is available for SAP employees and partners on learning.sap.com.

If you’re supporting Sales and Presales enablement, now is a good time to point your teams to it — and help them turn GRC from a compliance topic into a business conversation.

entry.title: E-invoicing France : field to store electronic address in BP
entry.content:
Dear Community,

Where values from Routing Code and Suffix/Addressing Line Code are stored in France eDocuments to S/4HANA Public Cloud?

How can we fulfill this tags and configure it accordingly?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: E-invoicing France : field to store electronic addres...
entry.content:
Hello!

In the context of France localization, the Routing Code and Suffix (Addressing Line Code) are handled through standard configuration in SAP solutions.

  • 1. Maintenance in Business Partner (BP) Master Data
    In the Business Partner Master Data, under the Identification tab, the following parameters can be maintained:
    FRADLC – France: Addressing Line Code
    FRROCO – France: Routing Code

When these parameters are maintained in the BP Master Data, they are considered active by the system.

  • 2. Maintenance in Billing Document Header Text
    Alternatively, the Suffix (Addressing Line Code) can also be maintained in the Billing Document Header Text.

This option is typically relevant in scenarios where multiple suffixes are active for the same Business Partner. In such cases, the value maintained at the Billing Header level takes precedence over the value defined in the Master Data.

entry.title: Material Quality Rejected should show as Rejected in Inventory Status
entry.content:
Right now when a material is rejected it is going to blocked stock.
Even for materials that we plan to use also is in blocked stock.
This needs a differentiation. Rejected should show as Rejected so that
we could easily track materials that are rejected and not returned. 
Now we are blocking the material ,  mark it as we can't use it or some times is not required to use in normal situations , objective is to separate this two case
1. Material that are purchased and rejected during QC check and we are blocking it - means it need to returned to vendor 
2. Material that we are purchased or produced, - it can use but not for all situations , now for this case also we are blocking the material 

Is there any option to separate the second case to any other stock type that we are not  normally used thus material easily in stock reports such as MMBE or MB52 , and do not allow to use it in process order or any other material consumption , allow all the transactions only after moving the material to unrestricted or blocked

entry.title: Inbound France eInvoice via DRC/PEPPOL not arriving in ERP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working on a France eInvoicing implementation in a R3-system using SAP DRC with PEPPOL for outbound and inbound invoices. We set up a test environment, in which a customer has the same identification numbers as our seinding system. Using this method in other implementations in the same system, we could send an eInvoice and receive it as well.

Current situation:

  • Outbound invoices (France) are successfully generated in the eDocument Cockpit.
  • In the BTP PEPPOL Exchange, the invoice is visible for our Participant
  • The Participant shows Send and Receive activated in the PEPPOL Exchange UI. -> Sending invoices works as intended
  • In the PEPPOL exchange app in "Monitor documents" it says "Document pushed" regarding a receiving document
  • No inbound eInvoice arrives in our ERP (eDocument Cockpit remains empty)

The question is: Are there special France-specific requirements for incoming invoices? We checked the implemented customizing and master data, but haven't found anything suspicious. The configuration took place according to the sap documentation.

Any hints, configuration tips, or known issues about how inbound eInvoices should be set up for France would be highly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Cascading Effect between Inputs Controls is not working with Linked Analysis for different charts
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently facing a limitation in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) while building a Story report, and I would like to share it here — both to confirm if others are experiencing the same issue and to ask for your support.

The Scenario:

In our project, the customer requires cascading behavior between Input Controls that are connected to different charts using Linked Analysis.

The expected behavior would be:

  • Input Control A filters Chart A
  • Input Control B filters Chart B
  • And selections in one control should cascade and dynamically restrict the available values in the other control

However, according to SAP Help:

“There is no cascading effect between group filters that affect different sets of widgets.”

So even when Linked Analysis is configured, cascading does not work if the input controls affect different widget sets.

The Impact:

This limitation makes it impossible to implement a very common reporting requirement:

  • Dependent dropdown filters
  • Context-driven selections across different sections of a Story
  • Controlled and guided user navigation experience

For customers coming from BW/BO or other BI tools, this behavior is often expected as standard.

Existing Improvement Request:

There is already an Improvement Request submitted to SAP for this functionality.

However, for the request to be reviewed by SAP Product Management, it must receive at least 25 votes from different customers/companies.

Without enough votes, the request will not move forward for evaluation.

Call for Support:

If you:

  • Have faced the same limitation
  • Believe this functionality would improve SAC usability
  • Or simply want to support the enhancement

Please consider voting for the improvement request.

The more visibility and votes it receives, the higher the chance SAP will analyze and potentially prioritize it.

The link to vote in this request are: Request 

Let’s see if we can reach the required 25 votes and bring this topic to SAP’s attention.

Thanks in advance for your support!

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence for SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Feature requests should not be made via a Customer Support Ticket.
The channel for requesting new features in SAP Cloud ALM is the SAP Continuous Influence for SAP Cloud ALM!

 

What is SAP Continuous Influence for SAP Cloud ALM

  • SAP Continuous Influence is a customer engagement program that enables customers to submit improvement requests and vote on enhancements for SAP Cloud ALM features and functionality.
  • It serves as the official channel for customers to influence the development roadmap and prioritize new capabilities across SAP Cloud ALM's Implementation, Operations, and Service components.

Components

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation: Campaign for project management, testing, and deployment enhancements
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Operations: Campaign for monitoring, automation, and operational improvements
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Service: Campaign for service delivery experience and collaboration features

Key Features

  • Voting System: Requests with 10 or more votes are evaluated and reviewed by SAP coaches for potential inclusion in the development roadmap
  • Direct Access Portal: Customers can access influence campaigns through the SAP Influence platform at influence.sap.com
  • Transparent Process: Clear status tracking and updates on submitted requests, including delivered, planned, or declined outcomes

Process Flow

  1. Submit Requests: Customers identify gaps or desired enhancements in SAP Cloud ALM functionality and submit improvement requests through the appropriate campaign 
  2. Community Voting: Other customers review and vote on submitted requests that would be helpful for their organizations
  3. SAP Evaluation: Requests meeting the voting threshold are evaluated by SAP coaches and development teams for feasibility and strategic alignment
  4. Roadmap Integration: Approved requests are incorporated into the SAP Cloud ALM development roadmap with planned delivery timelines
    SAP Cloud ALM Roadmap Viewer

Benefits for SAP Cloud ALM

  • Customer-Driven Development: Ensures product evolution aligns with real customer needs and use cases
  • Enhanced User Experience: Continuous improvements based on actual user feedback improve overall platform usability
  • Strategic Prioritization: Voting mechanism helps SAP prioritize features that will benefit the largest number of customers 

The SAP Cloud ALM Component Campaigns can be easily accessed directly from the SAP Cloud ALM Home Page

 

 

 

SAP Cloud ALM has 3 campaigns running and you are invited to submit and vote on enhancement requests: Below are the landing page images and you can see the number of requests that have been submitted. You can vote up existing requests or create your own based on your business needs or feature requirements.

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation  https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3501

     

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Operations             https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3525

     

  • SAP Cloud ALM for Service                     https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3669

     

For further information you can refer to KBA #3391676 which also has links to the campaigns.

Hence in a nutshell, Customer Support Tickets are not able to request new Features, so please use the SAP Continuous Influence for SAP Cloud ALM to request New Features that your company would like to see in the product.
